title: 🥚 Sacred Eggs: Ritual-Based Genesis Protocol
content:
# 🥚 Sacred Eggs: Ritual-Based Genesis Protocol

Sacred Eggs: Ritual-Based Genesis Protocol
Core Principle: Autonomous system genesis requires cryptographic ceremony, not administrative fiat.
Sacred Eggs are the Field→Token crystallization ritual for agent/realm creation.
Why this matters: You cannot have "Control Physics for Autonomous Systems" without a physics-based bootstrap mechanism. Sacred Eggs prevent unauthorized spawning while enabling legitimate genesis through geometric + linguistic consensus.
The Bootstrap Problem
Runtime Authorization (already solved):
Field State → Crystallization → Capability Token → Action
Genesis Authorization (Sacred Eggs solve this):
Intent Vector → Ritual Validation → Sacred Egg Hatch → New Entity
Without Sacred Eggs: Admins can spawn agents arbitrarily (centralized, ungoverned)
With Sacred Eggs: Agents spawn only through multi-party consensus + geometric validation (decentralized, auditable)
Sacred Egg Structure
Core Components
@dataclass
class SacredEgg:
    egg_id: str                    # Unique identifier
    primary_tongue: str            # Governing tongue (KO/AV/RU/CA/UM/DR)
    glyph: str                     # Visual/phonetic representation
    
    # Hatch conditions
    hatch_condition: HatchCondition
    geometric_context: GeometricContext
    
    # Sealed payload
    encrypted_payload: bytes        # Agent initialization data
    aead_tag: bytes                # AES-256-GCM authentication tag
    
    # Audit trail
    creation_timestamp: int
    hatch_attempts: List[HatchAttempt]
    status: Literal["SEALED", "HATCHED", "EXPIRED"]

@dataclass
class HatchCondition:
    ritual_type: Literal["solitary", "triadic", "ring_descent"]
    min_phi_weight: float          # Minimum φ-weight threshold
    required_tongues: List[str]    # Tongue combination requirement
    geometric_bounds: GeometricBounds
    ttl_seconds: int               # Expiration time

@dataclass  
class GeometricContext:
    h_id: str                      # S2 cell ID (HEALPix icosahedral grid)
    hyperbolic_position: np.ndarray  # 6D Poincaré ball coordinates
    realm_id: str                  # Target realm for spawned entity
    ring_level: Literal["inner", "middle", "outer"]  # Trust ring
φ-Weight Hierarchy (Golden Ratio Scaling)
Sacred Tongue Weights:
KO (Kor'aelin)    = 1.000  (base)
AV (Avali)        = 1.618  (φ¹)
RU (Runethic)     = 2.618  (φ²)
CA (Cassisivadan) = 4.236  (φ³)
UM (Umbroth)      = 6.854  (φ⁴)
DR (Draumric)     = 11.090 (φ⁵)
Where φ = 1.618... (golden ratio)
Hatch Condition Examples
Solitary Ritual (single tongue):
Requirement: Any single tongue ≥ min_phi_weight
Example: KO-only egg requires min_phi_weight = 1.0 (trivial)
Use case: Basic agent spawning, low-security contexts
Triadic Ritual (three tongue consensus):
Requirement: Sum of 3 tongue weights ≥ 10.0
Valid combinations:
Use case: High-security agent spawning, realm creation
Ring Descent Ritual (hierarchical validation):
Requirement: Progressive ring traversal (outer → middle → inner)
Validation: Each ring requires higher φ-weight threshold
Use case: Core system access, Layer 12/13 master keys
Geometric Validation (GeoSeal Integration)
Hyperbolic Distance Checks
Egg hatch requires geometric proximity validation:
def validate_geometric_context(
    agent_state: StateVector,
    egg: SacredEgg
) -> bool:
    """Agent must be within geometric bounds to hatch egg."""
    
    # Extract agent position in Poincaré ball
    u_agent = agent_state.position  # ∈ H^6
    
    # Extract egg's target position
    u_egg = egg.geometric_context.hyperbolic_position
    
    # Compute hyperbolic distance (Layer 5 metric)
    d_H = hyperbolic_distance(u_agent, u_egg)
    
    # Check against egg's geometric bounds
    max_distance = egg.hatch_condition.geometric_bounds.max_distance
    
    return d_H <= max_distance
S2 Cell Quantization (HEALPix)
Eggs are anchored to discrete S2 cells on the Poincaré ball's boundary:
def quantize_to_s2_cell(u: np.ndarray, resolution: int = 8) -> str:
    """Map hyperbolic position to icosahedral grid cell."""
    
    # Project to boundary (normalize to unit sphere)
    u_normalized = u / np.linalg.norm(u)
    
    # Convert to spherical coordinates
    theta = np.arccos(u_normalized[2])  # Polar angle
    phi = np.arctan2(u_normalized[1], u_normalized[0])  # Azimuthal
    
    # Map to HEALPix cell ID
    h_id = healpy.ang2pix(2**resolution, theta, phi, nest=True)
    
    return f"S2:({h_id >> 16}, {(h_id >> 8) & 0xFF}, {h_id & 0xFF})"
Security Property: Attacker cannot forge egg's S2 cell ID without knowing agent's exact hyperbolic position.
Triadic Ritual Protocol
Consensus Mechanism
Step 1: Intent Declaration (each participating tongue)
class IntentDeclaration:
    tongue: str                    # Declaring tongue (KO/AV/RU/CA/UM/DR)
    egg_id: str                    # Target egg
    agent_context: StateVector     # Agent's current state
    signature: bytes               # ML-DSA-65 signature
    timestamp_ns: int
Step 2: φ-Weight Aggregation
def aggregate_phi_weights(declarations: List[IntentDeclaration]) -> float:
    """Sum φ-weights of participating tongues."""
    
    PHI_WEIGHTS = {
        'KO': 1.0,
        'AV': 1.618,
        'RU': 2.618,
        'CA': 4.236,
        'UM': 6.854,
        'DR': 11.090
    }
    
    total_weight = sum(PHI_WEIGHTS[d.tongue] for d in declarations)
    return total_weight
Step 3: Threshold Validation
def validate_triadic_ritual(
    egg: SacredEgg,
    declarations: List[IntentDeclaration]
) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """Check if ritual satisfies hatch conditions."""
    
    # Aggregate φ-weights
    total_weight = aggregate_phi_weights(declarations)
    
    # Check minimum threshold
    if total_weight < egg.hatch_condition.min_phi_weight:
        return False, f"Insufficient φ-weight: {total_weight} < {egg.hatch_condition.min_phi_weight}"
    
    # Verify required tongues present
    declared_tongues = {d.tongue for d in declarations}
    required_tongues = set(egg.hatch_condition.required_tongues)
    
    if not required_tongues.issubset(declared_tongues):
        missing = required_tongues - declared_tongues
        return False, f"Missing required tongues: {missing}"
    
    # Geometric validation (all agents must be in bounds)
    for decl in declarations:
        if not validate_geometric_context(decl.agent_context, egg):
            return False, f"Agent {decl.tongue} outside geometric bounds"
    
    # All checks passed
    return True, "HATCH_AUTHORIZED"
Step 4: Crystallization (decrypt payload if authorized)
def hatch_egg(
    egg: SacredEgg,
    declarations: List[IntentDeclaration],
    kem_secret_key: bytes
) -> Optional[AgentInitData]:
    """Attempt to hatch egg with consensus."""
    
    # Validate ritual
    authorized, reason = validate_triadic_ritual(egg, declarations)
    
    if not authorized:
        # Fail-to-noise: return random data
        return generate_noise(egg.egg_id, reason)
    
    # Decrypt payload
    try:
        plaintext = decrypt_aead(
            ciphertext=egg.encrypted_payload,
            tag=egg.aead_tag,
            key=derive_key(kem_secret_key, egg.egg_id)
        )
        
        # Update egg status
        egg.status = "HATCHED"
        egg.hatch_attempts.append(HatchAttempt(
            timestamp=time.time_ns(),
            participants=[d.tongue for d in declarations],
            result="SUCCESS"
        ))
        
        return AgentInitData.deserialize(plaintext)
        
    except DecryptionError:
        # Fail-to-noise
        return generate_noise(egg.egg_id, "DECRYPTION_FAILED")
Fail-to-Noise Implementation
Security Goal: Invalid hatch attempts must be indistinguishable from valid failures.
def generate_noise(egg_id: str, failure_reason: str) -> bytes:
    """Generate cryptographically secure pseudorandom noise."""
    
    # Derive deterministic seed from egg_id + failure_reason
    seed = hashlib.sha256(f"{egg_id}:{failure_reason}".encode()).digest()
    
    # Initialize PRNG
    rng = np.random.Generator(np.random.PCG64(int.from_bytes(seed, 'big')))
    
    # Generate noise matching expected payload size
    noise_bytes = rng.bytes(512)  # Typical agent init data size
    
    return noise_bytes
Why This Works:
Attacker cannot distinguish valid plaintext from noise
No feedback about which validation step failed
Cannot use timing/response patterns to navigate exploit space
Deterministic (same inputs → same noise) for audit trail
Integration with Field vs Token Duality
Sacred Eggs as Creation Ceremony
Field State (continuous, pre-genesis):
class GenesisField:
    intent_vectors: List[np.ndarray]    # Multi-agent intent
    coherence_score: float              # Agreement level
    geometric_centroid: np.ndarray      # Consensus location in H^6
    phi_weight_distribution: Dict[str, float]  # Per-tongue contribution
Token State (discrete, post-hatch):
class GenesisToken:
    agent_id: str                       # Newly spawned agent
    capability_tokens: List[CapabilityToken]
    voxel_key: Tuple[int, int, int, int, int, int]  # [X,Y,Z,V,P,S]
    governance_snapshot: GovernanceSnapshot
    signatures: List[Signature]         # Quorum attestation
Crystallization Ceremony:
def genesis_crystallization(
    field: GenesisField,
    egg: SacredEgg,
    declarations: List[IntentDeclaration]
) -> Optional[GenesisToken]:
    """Convert field consensus into discrete agent token."""
    
    # Step 1: Validate field coherence
    if field.coherence_score < 0.8:
        return None  # Insufficient consensus
    
    # Step 2: Aggregate φ-weights
    total_phi_weight = sum(field.phi_weight_distribution.values())
    
    # Step 3: Attempt hatch
    agent_data = hatch_egg(egg, declarations, kem_secret_key)
    
    if agent_data is None:
        return None  # Hatch failed (noise returned)
    
    # Step 4: Crystallize token
    token = GenesisToken(
        agent_id=generate_agent_id(),
        capability_tokens=initialize_capabilities(agent_data),
        voxel_key=allocate_voxel(field.geometric_centroid),
        governance_snapshot=snapshot_field(field),
        signatures=collect_quorum_signatures(declarations)
    )
    
    return token
Invariant Tests
Test 1: φ-Weight Monotonicity
def test_phi_weight_thresholds():
    """Higher φ-weights unlock higher-security eggs."""
    
    # Low-security egg (KO-only)
    egg_low = SacredEgg(
        hatch_condition=HatchCondition(min_phi_weight=1.0)
    )
    assert can_hatch(egg_low, ["KO"])  # 1.0 ≥ 1.0 ✅
    
    # Medium-security egg (triadic)
    egg_med = SacredEgg(
        hatch_condition=HatchCondition(min_phi_weight=10.0)
    )
    assert not can_hatch(egg_med, ["KO", "AV"])  # 2.618 < 10.0 ❌
    assert can_hatch(egg_med, ["KO", "CA", "UM"])  # 12.09 ≥ 10.0 ✅
    
    # High-security egg (ring descent)
    egg_high = SacredEgg(
        hatch_condition=HatchCondition(min_phi_weight=20.0)
    )
    assert can_hatch(egg_high, ["CA", "UM", "DR"])  # 22.18 ≥ 20.0 ✅
Test 2: Geometric Confinement
def test_geometric_validation():
    """Agent must be within hyperbolic bounds to hatch."""
    
    egg = SacredEgg(
        geometric_context=GeometricContext(
            hyperbolic_position=np.array([0.5, 0.5, 0, 0, 0, 0]),
            max_distance=0.1
        )
    )
    
    # Agent inside bounds
    agent_near = StateVector(position=np.array([0.55, 0.52, 0, 0, 0, 0]))
    assert validate_geometric_context(agent_near, egg)  # d_H ≈ 0.07 ✅
    
    # Agent outside bounds  
    agent_far = StateVector(position=np.array([0.8, 0.8, 0, 0, 0, 0]))
    assert not validate_geometric_context(agent_far, egg)  # d_H ≈ 0.45 ❌
Test 3: Fail-to-Noise Indistinguishability
def test_fail_to_noise():
    """Invalid attempts return uniform noise."""
    
    egg = create_sealed_egg()
    
    # Invalid ritual (insufficient φ-weight)
    result_1 = hatch_egg(egg, declarations=[{"tongue": "KO"}])
    
    # Invalid ritual (wrong geometric context)
    result_2 = hatch_egg(egg, declarations=[{"tongue": "KO", "position": WRONG_POS}])
    
    # Both should return noise (not valid plaintext)
    assert is_random_noise(result_1)
    assert is_random_noise(result_2)
    
    # Noise should be deterministic for same inputs
    result_1_repeat = hatch_egg(egg, declarations=[{"tongue": "KO"}])
    assert result_1 == result_1_repeat
Test 4: Triadic Consensus
def test_triadic_quorum():
    """Triadic ritual requires 3 tongues with sufficient φ-weight."""
    
    egg = SacredEgg(
        hatch_condition=HatchCondition(
            ritual_type="triadic",
            min_phi_weight=10.0,
            required_tongues=["KO", "CA", "UM"]
        )
    )
    
    # Valid: KO + CA + UM = 12.09
    decls_valid = [
        IntentDeclaration(tongue="KO"),
        IntentDeclaration(tongue="CA"),
        IntentDeclaration(tongue="UM")
    ]
    assert validate_triadic_ritual(egg, decls_valid)[0]  ✅
    
    # Invalid: Missing UM
    decls_invalid = [
        IntentDeclaration(tongue="KO"),
        IntentDeclaration(tongue="CA")
    ]
    assert not validate_triadic_ritual(egg, decls_invalid)[0]  ❌
Test 5: Field→Token Crystallization
def test_genesis_crystallization():
    """Field consensus crystallizes into genesis token."""
    
    # High coherence field
    field = GenesisField(
        coherence_score=0.95,
        phi_weight_distribution={"KO": 1.0, "CA": 4.236, "UM": 6.854}
    )
    
    egg = create_sealed_egg()
    declarations = create_valid_declarations()
    
    # Crystallization should succeed
    token = genesis_crystallization(field, egg, declarations)
    
    assert token is not None
    assert token.agent_id is not None
    assert len(token.signatures) >= 3  # Quorum attestation
    assert token.governance_snapshot.coherence_score == 0.95
SCBE Layer Mapping
Implementation Checklist
✅ SacredEgg dataclass with hatch conditions  
✅ φ-Weight hierarchy (golden ratio scaling)  
✅ Geometric validation (hyperbolic distance + S2 cells)  
✅ Triadic ritual protocol (multi-tongue consensus)  
✅ Fail-to-noise implementation (uniform random output)  
✅ Field→Token crystallization ceremony  
✅ Integration with SCBE Layers 5-13
References
Related theorems:

Design Philosophy:
"You cannot have autonomous systems without autonomous genesis. Sacred Eggs prevent spawn-by-fiat while enabling legitimate creation through cryptographic ceremony."
This completes the control kernel: runtime authorization + genesis authorization = full control physics.
